subject: DevOps
title: Fastly's Global CDN Outage (2021)
description: A fast breakdown of Fastly's Global CDN Outage (2021) for builders.

hook.text: June 8, 2021. Reddit, Twitch, and the UK government site went down. Fastly's CDN had failed.

buildup.text: A customer toggled a config setting that triggered a latent software bug in Fastly's network.

discovery.text: The bug had existed for weeks. The specific config combination that triggered it was rare.

twist.text: 85% of Fastly's network returned errors. A single customer action broke a global CDN.

climax.text: Fastly fixed it in 49 minutes. They later deployed a fix for the underlying latent bug.

punchline.text: Latent bugs wait for triggers. Test rare config combinations.

quiz.question: What triggered the Fastly CDN outage in 2021?
options: A customer config change that hit a latent software bug
